data.title: Canada's Foreign Minister Says New Economic, Security Partners Needed Amid Shift in U.S.
data.description:
By Paul Vieira

OTTAWA--Canadian Foreign Minister Melanie Joly said her country needs deeper economic and security ties with Europe and parts of Asia amid the geopolitical shift unfolding under President Trump.

"We have not necessarily had the reflex to diversify trade, and we've been overreliant on the U.S.," Joly said Friday at a business event in Vancouver, British Columbia. "We need new alliances and new partnerships. We need to diversify, not only the trade side, but also on the security front."

Trump said this week he's on course to slap tariffs of up to 25% on imports from Canada starting March 4. Nearly 20% of Canada's gross domestic product is tied to exports to the U.S., and Bank of Canada officials have warned a prolonged trade conflict between Ottawa and Washington would have "severe" repercussions for the economy.

"When they negotiate with us, they don't only talk about trade, they actually talk about a series of different topics," Joly said of talks to avert tariffs on the country's U.S.-bound exports. She added Canadian officials have repeatedly warned U.S. officials and lawmakers that hefty tariffs on Canadian imports will result in higher prices for U.S. consumers and potential job losses, given the integration between the two economies.

Since Trump's tariff threats, Canadian politicians and business leaders have talked about the need to expand business opportunities with non-U.S. markets. Export Development Canada, the country's export-credit agency, told The Wall Street Journal this week it has witnessed a surge in interest from companies looking to explore new markets as they brace for tariffs.

"We're having conversations now with countries that we are not that close to," Joly told the Vancouver audience. "We're saying, 'We need to share intelligence. We need to do more defense cooperation. We need more of your trade, and we need also more of your knowledge.'"

Write to Paul Vieira at paul.vieira@wsj.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

February 28, 2025 15:17 ET (20:17 GMT)

Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Inflation didn't get any worse. When will it start going down again?
data.description:
By Jeffry Bartash

Rising prices are still a challenge for the Fed

Inflation is still a sore spot for the U.S. economy, but at least it didn't get much worse at the start of the year.

That's the best that can be said about the latest reading of the personal consumption expenditures price index, the Federal Reserve's preferred tool to track the inflation rate.

The PCE index rose a sharper-than-desired 0.3% in January, but it came in a few ticks cooler than the better-known consumer-price index.

Read: U.S. inflation rate doesn't get any worse, PCE shows, but latest rise to keep Fed sidelined

What's more, the 12-month rate of inflation slowed to 2.5% from 2.6%.

The PCE index is considered the most accurate measure of U.S. inflation. The Fed is aiming to bring down the rate of inflation to 2%, returning it close to low prepandemic levels.

The Fed isn't far off the mark, but its effort to tame inflation has suffered a setback since late last year.

The rate of inflation, in fact, had dropped to as low as 2.1% in September before heading higher again.

"Inflation is back under the Fed's microscope and is more stubborn than policy makers had hoped," said senior economist Ben Ayers of Nationwide.

Why is it so stubborn?

Blame a strong U.S. economy, for one thing: Strong demand for goods and service pushes up the price of supplies and labor.

Some inflation also appears to have gotten embedded in the economy, making it harder to eradicate.

Whatever the case, the rebound in inflation has forced the Fed to put further reductions in U.S. interest rates on ice. And it means that lending costs are likely to remain at punishing levels for home buyers and other borrowers.

The future path of inflation, meanwhile, has gotten murkier.

One of the chief threats is the potential that major tariffs imposed by President Donald Trump could raise the cost of many imported goods. Fed officials are watching closely to try to figure out how tariffs could affect prices.

"Higher tariffs will raise prices of imported goods as well as made-in-America manufacturers, whose producers can charge higher prices with less foreign competition," said chief economist Bill Adams of Comerica Bank in Dallas.

For now, the threat of tariffs is mostly that. The White House has hit China with new charges, but it has held off on other major moves for now.

Also read: Consumer spending posts first drop in 22 months, but it's probably not a bad omen for the economy

The more immediate focus will shift to the U.S. employment report for February, which comes out next Friday.

Wall Street DJIA SPX hopes to see slower growth in wages and mild employment growth, both of which would suggest that labor costs are not adding much to inflation.

-Jeffry Bartash

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  02-28-25 1518ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Trump and Musk's Social Security 'chainsaw massacre' is slashing all the wrong things
data.description:
By Brett Arends

Program cuts are penny-wise, pound-moronic

President Donald Trump's decision to apply his chainsaw to the Social Security Administration will do nothing to shore up the program's finances - or help balance the federal budget.

But it will almost certainly be bad news for retirees and those who need to claim disability.

Let me take the first point first. Why will it do nothing for the program? Because the numbers involved, as usual, are chicken feed.

Social Security was already being run on the cheap. According to the latest annual trustees' report, covering 2023, total administration costs amounted to $7.2 billion for the year. That was just 0.5% of total annual benefit payments, which totaled $1.38 trillion. And it was a mere 0.25% of total assets, which were $2.8 trillion.

In comparison, the private sector usually charges more than twice that, or 0.56% of assets, just to run a bond fund. That's without the enormous administrative burden of running America's pension plan and disability plan.

A financial planner, meanwhile, will probably charge you 1% of your assets.

Meanwhile, Trump's promised tax cuts are expected to cost at least $400 billion a year. That's 55 times greater than the entire cost of running Social Security, including Social Security Disability Insurance.

And the hole in Social Security's accounts is currently estimated at $22.6 trillion. That's more than 3,100 times the total annual administrative costs of the program.

So even closing down the program's administration completely wouldn't get us anywhere.

As so often happens, the public is being played like a banjo. A relentless stream of news announcements, each reporting real or imagined "efficiency gains," are giving the impression of deep cuts. But even the things that really are being cut or canceled are immaterial when it comes to the overall size of the budget - although they probably sound great to all those people too lazy to do their homework or check their facts.

Now for the second point: Cutting Social Security staff will have an effect on the people who use the program.

As it happens, not only is the Social Security Administration not overstaffed, it is grossly understaffed. It recently saw its staffing levels fall to their lowest levels in 25 years. This notwithstanding the vast increase in the U.S. population, and especially its elder population, since 2000.

Until the last few weeks, the Social Security Administration had been trying to rebuild its workforce.

There is a huge backlog of work. Worst affected are people applying for disability benefits, particularly those who received a denial and are appealing.

As of the end of 2023 there was a backlog of more than 320,000 cases. If that sounds bad, consider this: It was the lowest level since 2000. Those running the program had a target of getting the average wait time down to 270 days - that is, nine months.

(Note: As anyone who has studied for an insurance license or a financial-planning qualification knows, Social Security runs the toughest disability-insurance program out there. The payments are small, and in order to qualify, you must be unable to perform not only your traditional job, but any job at all. This is why one of the first items of advice you are likely to get from a financial planner is to look into getting private disability insurance.)

Meanwhile, a question mark now hangs over the 1,230 Social Security field offices around the country. These have traditionally been where you can go in person to get questions answered and help if needed.

Elon Musk and his so-called Department of Government Efficiency have already closed a number of those field offices, and questions remain about the others. The Social Security Administration could not immediately be reached for comment.

Nancy Altman, president of the nonprofit Social Security Works, points out that these field offices have been around since 1936 and are an important resource in communities around the country. They're where any of us can go to get help or advice in person - an option that is especially important for elderly people.

Moreover, Altman adds, field-office workers are often the ones who spot attempts to defraud Social Security or its beneficiaries. If Trump and Musk were truly concerned about efficiency, she says, they would open new field offices and properly staff the existing ones.

On the bright side, 90-year-old widows will now have the exciting opportunity to learn how to navigate an app or an online portal, or to deal with a call center, if something goes wrong with their Social Security checks. I guess there's a silver lining to every cloud.

-Brett Arends

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  02-28-25 1508ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: ATKR Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Atkore Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit with the Schall Law Firm
data.description:
ATKR Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Atkore Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit with the Schall Law Firm

ATKR Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Atkore Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit with the Schall Law Firm

PR Newswire

LOS ANGELES, Feb. 28, 2025

LOS ANGELES, Feb. 28, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- The Schall Law Firm, a national shareholder rights litigation firm, reminds investors of a class action lawsuit against Atkore Inc. ("Atkore" or "the Company") (NYSE: ATKR) violations of §§10(b) and 20(a) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and Rule 10b-5 promulgated thereunder by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Investors who purchased the Company's securities between February 1, 2024 and February 3, 2025, inclusive (the "Class Period"), are encouraged to contact the firm before April 23, 2025.

If you are a shareholder who suffered a loss, click here to participate.

We also encourage you to contact Brian Schall of the Schall Law Firm, 2049 Century Park East, Suite 2460, Los Angeles, CA 90067, at 310-301-3335, to discuss your rights free of charge. You can also reach us through the firm's website at www.schallfirm.com, or by email at bschall@schallfirm.com.

The class, in this case, has not yet been certified, and until certification occurs, you are not represented by an attorney. If you choose to take no action, you can remain an absent class member.

According to the Complaint, the Company made false and misleading statements to the market. Atkore artificially inflated the price of its PVC pipes by engaging in a price-fixing scheme. The Company enjoyed unsustainable financial benefits through this anticompetitive scheme. When this price-fixing scheme was revealed to the public, the Company's PVC pipe price decreased substantially. Based on these facts, the Company's public statements were false and materially misleading throughout the class period. When the market learned the truth about Atkore, investors suffered damages.

Join the case to recover your losses.

The Schall Law Firm represents investors around the world and specializes in securities class action lawsuits and shareholder rights litigation.

This press release may be considered Attorney Advertising in some jurisdictions under the applicable law and rules of ethics.

CONTACT:

The Schall Law Firm
Brian Schall, Esq.,
www.schallfirm.com
Office: 310-301-3335
info@schallfirm.com

data.title: RHA HEALTH SERVICES ATTAINS PERSON-CENTERED EXCELLENCE ACCREDITATION FROM CQL
data.description:
RHA HEALTH SERVICES ATTAINS PERSON-CENTERED EXCELLENCE ACCREDITATION FROM CQL

RHA HEALTH SERVICES ATTAINS PERSON-CENTERED EXCELLENCE ACCREDITATION FROM CQL

PR Newswire

ASHEVILLE, N.C., Feb. 28, 2025

ASHEVILLE, N.C., Feb. 28, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- RHA Health Services is pleased to announce that it has achieved Person-Centered Excellence Accreditation from CQL | The Council on Quality and Leadership. This prestigious accomplishment demonstrates that RHA aligns with the highest standards in the human services field and recognizes the organization's significant impact on the lives of people with disabilities.

"This is a great achievement, and we're honored to partner with RHA on their accreditation!" says Mary Kay Rizzolo, President and CEO of CQL. "They're showing people supported, families, employees, and others, that they're committed to quality services that help people's dreams come true."

Since 1989, RHA has partnered with and celebrated people achieving their personal goals, building meaningful connections, experiencing positive outcomes in health and wellness, and more. Throughout Georgia, New Jersey, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, and Tennessee, RHA provides care to individuals with intellectual/developmental disabilities. In North Carolina and Pennsylvania, the organization also provides behavioral health services to individuals with mental health and substance use needs and provides services to those who are Deaf and Hard of Hearing. 

Person-Centered Excellence Accreditation, a four-year term, is awarded to organizations that meet CQL's rigorous standards, integrate CQL tools and approaches, and establish a quality management system to drive data-based decision-making. The accreditation process involves the validation of a Basic Assurances® Self-Assessment, implementation of the Personal Outcome Measures®, and alignment with Shared Values, along with various planning meetings, visits, focus groups, and consultation with CQL staff members. Concluding the initial accreditation review, RHA held a stakeholder event with a diverse group of participants to collaborate on a plan establishing action steps for reaching organizational goals.

"Every day, our employees are dedicated to creating safe and healthy environments while supporting the personal goals of the people we serve. Meeting CQL's high accreditation standards shows that at RHA, the people we serve and support are at the very center of everything we do," says Mickey Atkins, CEO of RHA.

To learn more about RHA, visit rhahealth.org.

About RHA Health Services
Founded in 1989, RHA began as a small provider of services to people with intellectual and developmental disabilities in North Carolina. RHA has expanded to provide disability services in Georgia, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, and Tennessee. They have added behavioral health services in North Carolina and Pennsylvania, and they also provide services for those who are Deaf and Hard of Hearing.

About CQL | The Council on Quality and Leadership
Since 1969, CQL | The Council on Quality and Leadership has been a leader in working with human service organizations and systems to continuously define, measure, and improve quality of life and quality of services for youth, adults, and older adults with intellectual and developmental disabilities, and psychiatric disabilities. CQL offers accreditation, training, certification, research, and consultation services to agencies that share our vision of dignity, opportunity, and community for all people.

Contact Information:
Krissy Stanley-Wood
RHA Health Services
VP of Product Development
& Integrations
marketing@rhanet.org

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/rha-health-services-attains-person-centered-excellence-accreditation-from-cql-302388953.html

SOURCE RHA Health Services

data.title: Which MySmile Teeth Whitening Product Is Right for You? A Guide to Brighter Smiles
data.description:
Which MySmile Teeth Whitening Product Is Right for You? A Guide to Brighter Smiles

Which MySmile Teeth Whitening Product Is Right for You? A Guide to Brighter Smiles

PR Newswire

LOS ANGELES, Feb. 28, 2025

LOS ANGELES, Feb. 28, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Achieving a whiter, brighter smile has never been easier with MySmile's range of effective teeth whitening solutions. Whether you're looking for an all-in-one kit, convenient whitening strips, or a simple one-step solution, MySmile has the perfect product to fit your lifestyle. But which one is right for you? Let's break it down.

1. MySmile Teeth Whitening Kit – Professional Results at Home

For those seeking a powerful, long-term whitening solution, the MySmile Teeth Whitening Kit is the ultimate choice. This kit includes:

  • A LED light accelerator for enhanced whitening
  • Pre-filled whitening gel syringes with enamel-safe ingredients
  • A flexible mouth tray for even coverage

This kit is perfect for individuals looking for professional-level whitening results without the hassle or expense of in-office treatments. The advanced formula is designed to remove tough stains from coffee, tea, wine, and smoking. Simply apply the gel, insert the LED light, and relax for just 10 minutes per session to see visible results within a few uses.

2. MySmile Whitening Strips – Convenient & Flexible Whitening

If you prefer a hassle-free, on-the-go whitening option, MySmile Whitening Strips are an excellent choice. These easy-to-use strips are designed to mold comfortably to your teeth while delivering powerful whitening action. Key features include:

  • 30-minute application time
  • Flexible, stretchable design for a snug fit
  • Strong adhesion to stay in place

These whitening strips are ideal for busy individuals who want a convenient way to brighten their smile without the need for extra tools. However, the strips may feel slightly scratchy to some users due to their strong grip, ensuring they stay securely in place during treatment.

3. MySmile Whitening Strips – Convenient & Flexible Whitening

For those who want a quick and easy whitening solution with minimal steps, the MySmile Whitening Mouth Trays provide a perfect alternative. With pre-filled whitening gel already in the trays, all you need to do is insert them into your mouth for 30 minutes and let them work their magic. Benefits include:

  • No mixing or additional application steps
  • Pre-measured gel for consistent whitening
  • Designed for comfort and ease of use

These mouth trays are perfect for first-time users or those who prefer a no-fuss, no-mess approach to teeth whitening.

Which MySmile Whitening Solution Is Best for You?

  • Go for the Teeth Whitening Kit if you want fast, professional-grade whitening with the help of LED light technology.
  • Choose Whitening Strips if you need a flexible, travel-friendly option that works in just 30 minutes.
  • Pick the Whitening Mouth Trays if you prefer an ultra-simple application with pre-filled trays for maximum convenience.

No matter which option you choose, MySmile ensures safe, non-sensitive, effective, and enamel-friendly whitening to help you achieve your brightest smile.

A Word from Our Founder

Bobby Jacobs, Founder of MySmile, shares, "At MySmile, we believe that everyone deserves a confident, radiant smile. That's why we've designed our whitening solutions to be effective, easy to use, and safe for your enamel. Whether you prefer a full kit or a simple strip, we have the perfect option to fit your lifestyle."

What Our Customers Say

"I've tried so many whitening products before, but MySmile's Whitening Kit is by far the best! After just a few uses, my teeth were visibly whiter, and the LED light made such a difference." – Jessica R., Verified Customer

For more information, visit www.mysmile.com or follow @mysmile on Instagram for the latest updates and exclusive offers.

Media Contact:
Winnie T.
Digital Marketing Manager, MySmile
winnie@mysmilesteeth.com

About MySmile

MySmile is dedicated to providing high-quality, dentist-approved teeth whitening solutions that are safe, effective, and easy to use. With a commitment to innovation and customer satisfaction, MySmile products help individuals achieve a radiant smile from the comfort of their home.

SOURCE MySmile Oral Care Inc.

data.title: University of Phoenix Hosts 2025 SXSW EDU Panel on Role of Skills in Transforming Education and Employment
data.description:
University of Phoenix Hosts 2025 SXSW EDU Panel on Role of Skills in Transforming Education and Employment

University leaders Jay Titus and Kent Blumberg join industry leaders Sara Francois and Freddy Mathew to share insights on how innovative collaborations of educators and employers close skills gaps

University of Phoenix is pleased to be a sponsor at 2025 SXSW EDU and host the panel, “Skills IRL: Transforming Education & Employment,” featuring Jay Titus, vice president and general manager of Workforce Solutions, Kent Blumberg, Ph.D., SHRM-SCP, national account manager and faculty at the University, Sara Francois, Vice President of Training at Optimum, and Freddy Mathew, Vice President, Data Management at Comcast, on March 3, 2025, 1:00-2:00pm CT in Austin, Texas.

The panel, titled “Skills IRL: Transforming Education & Employment,” will explore how innovative collaborations of educators and employers help break the tension of talent stagnation between employers and workers and engage meaningful talent development for the workforce. “Employers and educators make great collaborators,” states Titus. “Working adults know they need to build skills to move forward on their career paths, and employers know what skills are required to achieve organizational success. Educators can be the bridge between the two and empower organizations and workers to pursue growth and opportunity together.”

The 2025 SXSW EDU event brings together leading minds in education to share ideas, network, and create the future of teaching and learning. The 2025 SXSW EDU Conference & Festival celebrates innovation and learning in the education industry offering a wide range of sessions, in-depth workshops, learning experiences, mentorship, film screenings, future-focused competitions, an expo, networking opportunities, performances, and is a component of the South by Southwest® (SXSW®) family of conferences and festivals.

In his role as Vice President and General Manager of Workforce Solutions at University of Phoenix, Titus provides leadership and strategic oversight of the teams supporting workforce development and upskilling solutions employers nationwide. He previously held similar roles at Bridgepoint Education, and at the Academic Services and University Partnerships area of EdAssist, a division of Bright Horizons Family Solutions. Over the years, his contributions in the adult education space have helped hundreds of employers re-frame tuition reimbursement benefits to positively impact recruitment, retention and employee engagement. Titus holds an MS in Management from Lasell College and a bachelor’s in business administration from Bryant University.

As Vice President of Training at Optimum, Francois leads the transformation of frontline employee development through engaging learning strategies. With a career in Learning & Development spanning financial services, high-tech, residential real estate, and telecommunications, she has driven large-scale skills initiatives, including leading workforce upskilling efforts at a private equity company. She holds a master’s in organizational management from University of Phoenix and a bachelor’s degree in business at Stephen F. Austin State University.

An experienced people leader with over 20 years of experience in data systems, process assurance, and IT auditing, Mathew previously worked at PricewaterhouseCoopers as a Senior Consultant and at Avery Dennison as an IT Auditor. He began his career as a Systems Analyst at Unisys. Mathew holds a bachelor’s degree in finance and a MIS from Villanova University.

Blumberg is national account manager, Workforce Solutions, and senior faculty in the College of Business and Information Technology at University of Phoenix, currently teaching management and human resources courses in the Master of Business Administration program. Blumberg is trained and certified as an executive coach and built a consultancy in organizational development and executive coaching. He earned his doctorate in Leadership Development and Workforce Education from Louisiana State University, a Master of Science in Management from Marylhurst University and a Bachelor of Science in Mechanical Engineering from Stanford University.

Learn more here about the conference.

About University of Phoenix

University of Phoenix innovates to help working adults enhance their careers and develop skills in a rapidly changing world. Flexible schedules, relevant courses, interactive learning, skills-mapped curriculum for our bachelor’s and master’s degree programs and a Career Services for Life® commitment help students more effectively pursue career and personal aspirations while balancing their busy lives. For more information, visit phoenix.edu.

MEDIA CONTACT:
Sharla Hooper
University of Phoenix
sharla.hooper@phoenix.edu

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250228887302/en/

data.title: CYBEX INITIATES VOLUNTARY SAFETY RECALL OF SELECT ATON G AND ATON G SWIVEL CAR SEATS MANUFACTURED BEFORE MAY 10th, 2024
data.description:
CYBEX INITIATES VOLUNTARY SAFETY RECALL OF SELECT ATON G AND ATON G SWIVEL CAR SEATS MANUFACTURED BEFORE MAY 10th, 2024

CYBEX INITIATES VOLUNTARY SAFETY RECALL OF SELECT ATON G AND ATON G SWIVEL CAR SEATS MANUFACTURED BEFORE MAY 10th, 2024

PR Newswire

BOSTON, Feb. 28, 2025

Company is Committed to Safety and Helping Parents Address Affected Units

BOSTON, Feb. 28, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- CYBEX, a leading manufacturer and marketer of infant and juvenile products, today initiated a voluntary safety recall (NHTSA 25C002) of select CYBEX Aton G infant car seats after determining certain actions can allow the harness anchor pin to dislodge when the seat is not in use. CYBEX is providing owners of affected Aton G and Aton G Swivel child restraint systems and associated travel systems a product update kit, free of charge, that includes a device that locks the harness, and the harness retention pins in place, along with instructions for its installation.

There have been no reports to CYBEX of injuries to children related to this defect; however, CYBEX urges all consumers to install the harness anchor retention device once they are available.

Issue:
CYBEX has identified a potential defect with the Aton G infant car seat which is used in both Aton G and Aton G Swivel child restraint systems. If the seat is slid or scraped over a sharp edge (like a table or countertop) or possibly the edge of the Aton G Swivel Base, the harness anchorage hooks can bend. This bending can cause the harness anchor pin to dislodge when the seat is not in use. 

Key Points: 

  • When a child is secured in the harness, the anchor pins cannot come loose. 
  • If the anchor hooks bend, the harness strap can detach when not in use. 
  • Caregivers will notice any harness detachment when securing a child and checking harness tightness. 
  • If the harness anchor pin is missing, the harness will detach from the seat at the hip anchor.  In the event of a vehicle crash, the detached harness would not properly restrain the occupant, increasing the occupant's risk of injury.

Remedy:
CYBEX will provide all owners of affected Aton G infant car seats manufactured before May 10th, 2024, with a free product update kit on or about April 4th, 2025. This kit includes a device that locks the harness and the harness retention pin in place, even if the hooks are bent, along with instructions on how to install the device. 

While waiting for the product update kit, caregivers can continue to use the infant car seat if they follow these steps:

  • Before each use, check the harness anchor retention hooks for any damage.
  • If any hooks are bent away from the harness anchor pin, do not use the seat and contact CYBEX immediately for assistance. 

The affected infant car seats, sold as a part of a child restraint system or travel system, have model numbers listed in the table below and were manufactured before May 10th, 2024. The model number and date of manufacture are printed on a label on the bottom of the seat.

Model Name

Model Number

Aton G

522001441

522001443

522001445

522001447

522004777

522004779

Aton G Swivel

522004167

522004169

522004171

522004173

522005099

522004781

522004783

Eos and Aton G Travel System

522003793

522003795

522003797

522003799

522003897

Eos and Aton G Swivel Travel System

522004805

522005355

The company requests that owners of affected infant car seats contact CYBEX for a free product update kit by calling 1-877-242-5676 between 8 am and 5 pm ET, Monday through Friday. Customers should not return seats to retailers where purchased. The Aton G and Aton G Swivel car seats affected by this recall can be used while consumers wait for the product improvement kit.  

More information and demonstration videos are available at https://www.cybex-online.com/en/us/safety-notices-recalls-us.html.   

ABOUT CYBEX
CYBEX is a leading manufacturer and marketer of infant and juvenile products. Safety is CYBEX's number one priority, which is why all products are designed, engineered and rigorously tested with the goal of truly creating a safer, more peaceful ride for both children and caregivers. Columbus Trading-Partners USA Inc ("CTP") is the United States distributor for CYBEX.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/cybex-initiates-voluntary-safety-recall-of-select-aton-g-and-aton-g-swivel-car-seats-manufactured-before-may-10th-2024-302388945.html

SOURCE CYBEX

data.title: XRP Ecosystem Readies for Massive Growth as BlocScale Launchpad Kick Off Projects Onboarding
data.description:
LIJA, Malta, Feb. 28, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- The XRP ecosystem is witnessing a pivotal transformation, as BlocScale, the first-ever IDO launchpad built on XRP Ledger, has officially begun onboarding new projects. This marks a major breakthrough for XRP, which, despite its top-tier blockchain infrastructure and global market position, has historically lacked a structured launchpad to facilitate fundraising and innovation.


With BlocScale now onboarding its first wave of projects, the narrative for XRP is changing, setting the stage for a wave of new innovations, increased network activity, and expanded utility.

BlocScale Onboarding Projects: A Transformative Move for XRP

BlocScale’s first-mover advantage in the XRP ecosystem is highly significant. Until now, projects looking to launch on XRP Ledger had to rely on fragmented, non-optimized solutions that did not fully support automated fundraising, liquidity management, and transparent governance.

With BlocScale launching its project onboarding, XRP finally has a native platform that gives Web3 startups, memecoins, DeFi platforms, and real-world businesses a structured way to raise capital—right within the XRP ecosystem.

JOIN BLOCSCALE SEED SALE

$BLOC Seed Round is Now Open – A Rare Opportunity for Early Investors

With BlocScale’s private sale fully subscribed, the $BLOC Seed Round is now live, offering investors an exclusive chance to acquire $BLOC tokens before broader exchange listings and additional platform developments.

Seed Sale Details:

Price: 1 XRP = 50 $BLOC

Total Allocation: 10,000,000 BLOC

Soft Cap: 50,000 XRP

Hard Cap: 200,000 XRP
